criteria.label: Define random variable, PMF, PDF and CDF and state which applies to discrete vs continuous X.
criteria.example: Explain that Y = spam label ∈ {0,1} uses a PMF, latency X in ms uses a PDF, and F(x) = P(X ≤ x) works for both; give one property each (sum to 1, integrate to 1, non-decreasing).

criteria.label: Compute probabilities from a given PMF or simple PDF by hand or with SciPy.
criteria.example: Given p(0)=0.2, p(1)=0.5, p(2)=0.3, compute P(X ≤ 1) = 0.7 and P(X > 1) = 0.3; for Uniform(0,10), compute P(2 ≤ X ≤ 5) = 0.3.

criteria.label: Explain why PDF values can exceed 1 and why P(X = x) = 0 for continuous X.
criteria.example: Show Uniform(0, 0.5) has f(x)=2 on (0,0.5) integrating to 1, and argue a single point has zero width so its integral—and hence probability—is zero.

criteria.label: Connect softmax/sigmoid model outputs to PMFs over label random variables and identify invalid distributions.
criteria.example: Given logits [2,1,0], compute softmax ≈ [0.665, 0.245, 0.090], verify sum ≈ 1, and explain why [0.6, 0.3, 0.2] would require renormalization before cross-entropy.
